mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% as of August 2011 reflects elevated post-crisis unemployment
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent labor slack
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE changes and 20-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative UNRATE changes produce positive SPX returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS directly reflects consumer demand strength. Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales predicts positive equity returns as it signals robust aggregate demand and earnings growth potential over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389502 million dollars in August 2011
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 52% of triggered periods, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and inflation measured by CPIAUCSL begins to accelerate, the real interest rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% and CPIAUCSL at 226.268 in August 2011
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with inflation concerns
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from the sum of individual FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL effects.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth measured by INDPRO reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. Declining industrial production signals supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.0419 in August 2011, reflecting post-crisis recovery plateau
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST and employment measured by PAYEMS jointly signal residential construction demand and labor market tightness. When both rise together, they predict positive equity returns as they indicate broad-based economic expansion over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises AND PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds 50 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 604 thousand units and PAYEMS at 131132 thousand persons in July 2011
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with synchronized housing and employment growth
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either component alone.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth measured by PAYEMS indicates labor market expansion and rising household incomes. Sustained month-over-month employment gains above 100 thousand persons predict positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as they signal consumer spending capacity.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds 100 thousand persons
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS momentum produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerating above 3% annualized rate combined with elevated unemployment above 8% creates stagflationary pressure that increases equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month change exceeds 3% AND UNRATE above 8%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% and CPIAUCSL at 226.268 in August 2011
regime_hint: stagflation risk regime with policy uncertainty
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or the interaction effect is not distinguishable from independent effects of inflation and unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 represents the broadest measure of economic expansion. Quarterly GDP growth below 1% annualized rate signals economic stagnation and weak earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13260.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2011, reflecting slowdown concerns
failure_condition: When GDP growth falls below 1% annualized, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC between GDP growth and forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
